goal: Explain why Play does not animate a freshly-loaded robot and get something moving without deep lectures.
thoughts: Confirm sim is actually running (timeline advancing), then check the usual suspects: no joint drives, articulation not instantiated, physics disabled. Plain English: Play only advances the clock; the robot needs a reason to move. Offer a template where the robot DOES move on Play -- that is the fastest unblock for a Tuesday demo.
code:
state = get_timeline_state()
# If state.is_playing is False: sim_control(action="play")
issues = diagnose_physics_error(
    scope_path="/World",
    checks=["articulation_root", "joint_drives", "physics_scene", "mass_properties"]
)
for iss in issues.findings:
    explain_error(
        error_text=iss.message,
        audience="beginner",
        include_fix=True
    )
# Fastest unblock:
load_scene_template(
    template_name="<MOVING_ROBOT_PRESET>",
    target_path="/World"
)
failure_modes: Dropping him into USD articulation schema docs
Telling him to write a controller script on day one
Missing that the robot has no joint drives so Play cannot animate it
